reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by providing cells with duplicate commitments in different orderings (e.g., [A,B,A,C] vs [A,B,C,A]), causing the Fiat-Shamir challenge hash to include different commitment prefixes despite identical cryptographic content, enabling selective forgery by searching for favorable challenge values.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594 PeerDAS specification requires Fiat-Shamir challenge hash to include all verifier-relevant inputs in canonical form. The implementation incorrectly uses the original commitments_bytes array instead of the deduplicated unique_commitments array.
